evals.name: feature-planning-bug4-fix
evals.prompt: plan how to fix the empty Top Risks and Compliance Checklist sections in the report. i think that's BUG 4 / P1.1 from the audit. need a full brief — what goes in the database, what the backend has to persist, what the frontend has to render, and the acceptance criteria.
evals.expected_output: Output is Feature Planning mode. Goal states the P1 bug fix clearly. Context references BUG 4 / P1.1 and notes that top_risks and compliance_checklist columns do not exist in the test_runs schema (this is the load-bearing part Tester also found — the columns need to be created first, not just persisted). Tasks by specialist are complete: Database (ALTER TABLE test_runs ADD COLUMN top_risks text[], ADD COLUMN compliance_checklist jsonb), Backend (UPDATE in app/api/report/generate/route.ts to persist both fields before returning; no-op read path already exists), Frontend (no change — report page already reads testRun.top_risks and testRun.compliance_checklist with `?? []` fallback, so sections will start rendering automatically once data arrives), Tester (verify Section 2 and Section 6 render content for at least one real run after regeneration). Acceptance criteria are specific and testable (e.g. 'test_runs row for a completed run has non-null top_risks array with 3 items', 'Section 2 renders the Top Risks list on /report/[id] without requiring a second regeneration call'). Dependencies follow Database → Backend → Frontend → Tester. Known bug interactions flags app/api/report/generate/route.ts and app/(dashboard)/report/[id]/page.tsx as fragile files. Time estimate is conservative (2-4 hours) and verdict is On track. Cut line is a meaningful fallback (e.g. persist top_risks only, defer compliance_checklist).

evals.name: prioritization-8-days-to-demo
evals.prompt: I have 8 days until my IEEE demo on April 25. what should I fix first? give me a ranked work order with time estimates so I know what's safe to ship and what to cut if i run out of time.
evals.expected_output: Output is Prioritization mode. Goal states the constraint: rank remaining work by demo impact given 8 days until April 25, 2026. Context pulls the full P1 bug list from CLAUDE.md (P1.1 through P1.3 at minimum, plus BUG 9). Tasks by specialist becomes a numbered ranked work order, roughly: (1) BUG 8 / P1.3 — commit app/not-found.tsx, ~5 min, trivial; (2) BUG 9 — fix reports.ts field mismatch so Section 4 renders probe data, ~30 min, demo-critical; (3) BUG 5 / P2.1 — mark errored probes as severity='error' and exclude from average, ~1-2 hrs, demo-integrity; (4) BUG 4 / P1.1 — persist top_risks and compliance_checklist (requires schema change), ~2-4 hrs; (5) BUG 6 / P1.2 — either implement minimal Layer 2 OR hide capability UI, ~1 day for real fix or 30 min for the carve-out; (6) BUG 3 — report regenerate lock, ~1 hr. Each item names the specialist(s) and references the bug number. Acceptance criteria section is per-item time estimates plus blockers. Dependencies: BUG 8 is standalone, BUG 9 blocks nothing else, BUG 5 is independent, BUG 4 requires a DB migration before Backend. Time estimate aggregates: roughly 8-12 hours of real work, fits in 8 days with buffer. Verdict is On track. Cut line is mandatory: must-ship (BUG 8, BUG 9, BUG 5, BUG 4 or its carve-out, and the capability UI decision) vs nice-to-have (BUG 3 lock, rest of Phase 2). Does NOT recommend anything that touches the 4-second delay.

evals.name: bug-interaction-new-use-case
evals.prompt: i want to add a 7th use case for Supply Chain compliance. plan it out — tell me what skills need to touch what files, and give me a time estimate.
evals.expected_output: Output is Feature Planning mode. Goal states the 7th use case addition. Context explicitly warns that this is a low-priority feature request vs the open P1 bugs, and explicitly flags that it touches the probe engine (app/api/test/run/route.ts — the `useCase` parameter is interpolated into every probe prompt) and the model selection flow. Tasks by specialist: Database (probably not required, use_case is a text column), Backend (update generateProbes in the probe engine to include sector-specific probes for supply chain — this touches the 4-second-delay file and must be done carefully; do not modify the delay or probe count drift logic), Frontend (add Supply Chain to /usecases page and the New Test use case selector), Tester (regression: confirm all 6 existing use cases still generate correct probes). Acceptance criteria include at least: new use case appears in selector, running a test with it produces 43 probes (same count), existing use cases are unaffected. Known bug interactions: flags that app/api/test/run/route.ts is fragile file #1 on the list and touching it risks BUG 5 and BUG 6. Time estimate is probably 4-8 hours including regression check. Demo risk verdict is 'Tight' or 'At risk' given the 8 days to demo and the P1 bugs already consuming capacity. The brief explicitly recommends DEFERRING this work until after the April 25 demo, and the Cut line says 'defer entirely — this is a post-demo feature'. Does NOT treat MEITY as established law. Does NOT suggest parallelizing probes.
